words.def: a group or type of people or things that are similar in a particular way
words.example: sort of somebody/something, ‘What sort of music do you like?’ ‘Oh, all sorts.’

words.def: located in the south or facing south; connected with or typical of the south part of the world or a region
words.example: the southern slopes of the mountains

words.def: the act of forming words correctly from individual letters; the ability to do this
words.example: the differences between British and American spelling

words.def: the amount of money that is spent by a government, an organization or a person
words.example: to increase/cut/reduce spending

words.def: having a strong taste because spices have been added to it
words.example: I don't like spicy food.

words.def: a small creature with eight thin legs. Many spiders spin webs to catch insects for food.
words.example: a poisonous spider

words.def: the part of a person that includes their mind, feelings and character rather than their body
words.example: You are underestimating the power of the human spirit to overcome difficulties.

words.def: involving speaking rather than writing; expressed in speech rather than in writing
words.example: spoken English

words.def: a large sports ground surrounded by rows of seats and usually other buildings
words.example: a football/sports stadium

words.def: all the workers employed in an organization considered as a group
words.example: medical/nursing/teaching/coaching staff

words.def: a collection of information shown in numbers
words.example: Analysis of crime statistics showed high levels of violent crime within the area.

words.def: a figure of a person or an animal in stone, metal, etc., usually the same size as in real life or larger
words.example: a bronze/marble statue

words.def: a period of staying; a visit
words.example: I enjoyed my stay in Prague.

words.def: not moving; calm and quiet
words.example: A fallen tree floated in the still water.

words.def: a large shop that sells many different types of goods
words.example: It's available at London's three biggest stores.

words.def: a person that you do not know
words.example: There was a complete stranger sitting at my desk.

words.def: the quality of being physically strong
words.example: He pushed against the rock with all his strength.

words.def: pressure or worry caused by problems in somebody’s life or by having too much to do
words.example: emotional/mental stress

words.def: long, thin material used for tying things together, made of several threads that have been twisted together; a piece of string used to fasten or pull something or keep something in place
words.example: a piece/length/ball of string

words.def: in a way that shows definite and serious opinions or beliefs
words.example: a strongly worded protest

words.def: a room where radio or television programmes are recorded and broadcast from, or where music is recorded
words.example: a television/recording studio

words.def: showing a lack of thought or good judgement
words.example: a stupid mistake/question/idea

words.def: in a way that achieves your aims or what was intended
words.example: She had already successfully completed these courses.

words.def: happening or done quickly and unexpectedly
words.example: News of his sudden and unexpected death came as a great shock.

words.def: quickly and unexpectedly
words.example: I suddenly realized what I had to do.

words.def: to be badly affected by a disease, pain, sad feelings, a lack of something, etc.
words.example: I hate to see animals suffering.

words.def: a set of clothes made of the same cloth, including a jacket and trousers or a skirt
words.example: She was wearing a grey business suit.

words.def: right or appropriate for a particular purpose or occasion
words.example: a suitable candidate

words.def: to give a summary of something
words.example: This essay briefly summarizes some of our approaches.

words.def: a short statement that gives only the main points of something, not the details
words.example: a news summary

words.def: an amount of something that is provided or available to be used
words.example: Advances in agriculture increased the food supply.

words.def: a person who supports a political party, an idea, etc.
words.example: a strong/loyal/staunch/ardent supporter

words.def: to think or believe that something is true or possible (based on the knowledge that you have)
words.example: Getting a visa isn't as simple as you might suppose.

words.def: used to show that you are almost certain of what you are saying and want other people to agree with you
words.example: Surely we should do something about it?

words.def: the outside or top layer of something
words.example: We need a flat, smooth surface to play the game on.

words.def: to continue to live or exist
words.example: She was the last surviving member of the family.

words.def: a period of time during which you swim
words.example: Let's go for a swim.

words.def: a change in your body or mind that shows that you are not healthy
words.example: Symptoms include a headache and sore throat.

words.def: a small computer that is easy to carry, with a large touch screen and usually without a physical keyboard
words.example: The company has launched its latest 10-inch tablet.

words.def: to carry or move something from one place to another
words.example: take something, Remember to take your coat when you leave.

words.def: a natural ability to do something well
words.example: The festival showcases the talent of young musicians.

words.def: having a natural ability to do something well
words.example: a talented player/musician/artist

words.def: a speech or lecture on a particular subject
words.example: I went to several interesting talks at the conference.

words.def: a long, narrow piece of material with a sticky substance on one side that is used for sticking things together
words.example: adhesive/sticky tape

words.def: the particular quality that different foods and drinks have that allows you to recognize them when you put them in your mouth
words.example: a sweet/salty/bitter/sour taste

words.def: money that you have to pay to the government so that it can pay for public services. People pay tax according to their income and businesses pay tax according to their profits. Tax is also often paid on goods and services.
words.example: to pay your taxes

words.def: the work of a teacher
words.example: She wants to go into teaching (= make it a career).

words.def: a particular way of doing something, especially one in which you have to learn special skills
words.example: management techniques

words.def: to be likely to do something or to happen in a particular way because this is what often or usually happens
words.example: Women tend to live longer than men.

words.def: a shelter made of a large sheet of canvas, nylon, etc. that is supported by poles and ropes fixed to the ground, and is used especially for camping
words.example: to put up/take down a tent

words.def: of or belonging to them
words.example: Theirs are the children with very fair hair.

words.def: a formal set of ideas that is intended to explain why something happens or exists
words.example: theory of something, the theory of evolution/relativity

words.def: used to introduce the logical result of something that has just been mentioned
words.example: He's only 17 and therefore not eligible to vote.

words.def: to this degree; so
words.example: It's about this high (= as high as I am showing you with my hands).

words.def: from one end or side of something to the other
words.example: Put the coffee in the filter and let the water run through.

words.def: in or into every part of something
words.example: They export their products to markets throughout the world.
